# Typography & Text {#sec-typography}

This chapter exercises the typographic system: fonts, heading levels, text elements, lists, block quotes, custom CSS classes, and inline math.

## Fonts {#sec-fonts}

The body font is **Luciole**, a sans-serif designed for low-vision readers.
Inline `code()` uses **Fira Code** with programming ligatures: `|>`, `<-`, `!=`, `->`.

## Heading levels

Headings are numbered down to level 4 (`number-depth: 4`).
Each level has a distinct indent and size defined in `theme-base.scss`.

### Level 3 heading

Paragraph with *emphasis*, **strong emphasis**, a [link to the Quarto docs](https://quarto.org), and an internal reference to @sec-typography.

#### Level 4 heading

Deepest numbered level.

## Lists

Unordered:

- First item
- Second item with a *nested* block:
    - Nested bullet one
    - Nested bullet two
- Third item

Ordered:

1. Initialise the R session
2. Load the tidyverse
3. Render the book

## Block quote

> A model is a simplified representation of reality, useful precisely because of what it omits.
>
> --- George Box (paraphrased)

## Inline math

Inline math: $y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x + \varepsilon$.

The `html-math-method: plain` option renders math with plain CSS — no MathJax.

## Custom CSS classes

The theme defines semantic inline classes for use in prose.
Apply them with Span syntax: `[text]{.class}`.

| Class | Role | Example |
|-------|------|---------|
| `.cb1` | Bold, primary colour | [primary label]{.cb1} |
| `.cb2` | Bold, secondary colour | [secondary label]{.cb2} |
| `.nb` | Bold, neutral | [neutral label]{.nb} |
| `.nc` | Monospace, neutral (column names, keys) | [species]{.nc} |
| `.str` | Monospace, string colour | ["Adelie"]{.str} |
| `.dig` | Monospace, numeric colour | [42]{.dig} |
| `.sm-em` | Italic emphasis | [note aside]{.sm-em} |

Example in prose: the column [species]{.nc} takes the value ["Adelie"]{.str}, ["Chinstrap"]{.str}, or ["Gentoo"]{.str}; mean body mass is [4202]{.dig} g.
The [primary]{.cb1} and [secondary]{.cb2} colours follow the SCSS palette; [muted labels]{.nb} use the neutral tone.
